title: 10 Herbal Creams For Frozen Shoulder
intro_desc: Herbal creams can help relieve the pain and stiffness of frozen shoulder. These creams are made from herbs that have anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease pain. When applied to the affected area, they help relax the muscles and improve mobility.  Herbal teas, on the other hand, can help treat frozen shoulder from the inside out. Drinking teas made from specific herbs can help reduce inflammation and relax the muscles, making it easier to move the shoulder joint. For example, Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and swelling. Eucalyptus globulus, or eucalyptus, has decongestant properties that can help reduce swelling and ease pain. Zingiber officinale, or ginger, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and stiffness.  Drinking these herbal teas can bring several benefits to your life. Firstly, they can help you manage pain and stiffness, making it easier to perform daily activities. Secondly, they can help you recover faster from frozen shoulder, reducing the need for prolonged treatment and rehabilitation. Lastly, they can help you maintain a healthy lifestyle by promoting relaxation and reducing stress.  When using herbal teas to treat frozen shoulder, it's essential to note that they should be consumed in moderation and under the guidance of a healthcare professional. Additionally, it's crucial to combine them with other treatments, such as physical therapy and medication, as prescribed by a doctor.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains a number of bioactive constituents, including glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and triterpenes. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with frozen shoulder. Glycyrrhizin, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the production of inflammatory mediators, such as prostaglandins, which contribute to the pain and stiffness of frozen shoulder. The antioxidant properties of flavonoids and triterpenes can also help protect against oxidative stress and tissue damage. By reducing inflammation and promoting tissue health, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams may help alleviate symptoms of frozen shoulder and support the healing process.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for frozen shoulder
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup distilled water, 2 tablespoons glycerin, 2 tablespoons glycyrrhiza glabra root powder, 2 tablespoons coconut oil, 1 tablespoon beeswax
Heat distilled water in a small saucepan over low heat
Add glycerin, glycyrrhiza glabra root powder, and coconut oil to the saucepan. Stir until powder dissolves
Add beeswax to the saucepan and stir until melted. Remove from heat
Let the mixture cool and thicken. Pour into a container and store in the fridge

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams contains several bioactive constituents, including eucalyptol, camphor, and 1,8-cineole. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce pain and swelling in the affected shoulder joint. Eucalyptol also has a cooling effect, which can help numb the area and provide relief from pain. Camphor helps to reduce inflammation and relieve pain by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body. By applying Eucalyptus globulus creams to the affected area, you can reduce pain and inflammation, and improve mobility in your frozen shoulder.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for frozen shoulder
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of olive oil, and 10 drops of Eucalyptus globulus essential oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 30-second intervals, stirring between each interval until melted.
Add shea butter and olive oil to the melted mixture and stir until combined.
Remove the mixture from heat and stir in Eucalyptus globulus essential oil. Let it cool until it reaches a temperature of around 120°F to 130°F.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely before using. Store in a cool, dry place and apply to frozen shoulder 2-3 times a day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains active constituents like gingerol and shogaol, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, making them beneficial for reducing pain and swelling associated with frozen shoulder. The warming effect of ginger can also increase blood circulation, helping to reduce stiffness and promote healing in the affected area. Additionally, the bioactive compounds in Zingiber officinale creams have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which can contribute to the development of frozen shoulder. Regular use of Zingiber officinale creams may help to alleviate symptoms of frozen shoulder and improve range of motion.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for frozen shoulder
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of grated fresh ginger root (Zingiber officinale), 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 1 tablespoon of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler over low heat. Stir until beeswax is fully dissolved.
Remove the double boiler from heat and let it cool for 5 minutes. Add shea butter and stir until it's fully incorporated.
Add grated ginger root and vitamin E oil to the mixture. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and harden. Once hardened, your ginger cream is ready to use for frozen shoulder relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams contains bioactive constituents such as cineole and terpinen-4-ol, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help reduce swelling and pain in the affected area, making it easier to move the shoulder and improving range of motion. The creams also contain antioxidants like vitamin E and beta-carotene, which protect the skin from further damage and promote healing. The anti-inflammatory compounds in Melaleuca alternifolia creams may also help reduce stiffness and improve joint mobility, making it a potentially effective treatment for frozen shoulder. Regular application of the creams to the affected area may help alleviate symptoms and promote recovery.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for frozen shoulder
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of beeswax, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt beeswax, coconut oil, and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Stir until smooth.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool slightly. Add Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil and vitamin E oil. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into small jars or containers. Let it cool and solidify completely.
Apply a thin layer of the cream to the affected area of the frozen shoulder 2-3 times a day. Massage gently to help reduce pain and inflammation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Arnica montana creams contains boiactive constituents like thymol, sesquiterpene lactones, and flavonoids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the affected area. The sesquiterpene lactones, specifically helenalin and iso-helenalin, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. This helps to reduce the inflammation and pain associated with frozen shoulder. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Arnica montana creams may help to alleviate the symptoms of frozen shoulder, making it easier to move and perform daily activities.
remedies.image_alt: Arnica montana herbal creams for frozen shoulder
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of Arnica montana oil, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler over low heat.
Add the Arnica montana oil and vitamin E oil to the melted mixture. Stir well.
Remove from heat and let cool until the mixture thickens. Stir occasionally.
Pour the mixture into a jar and let it cool completely before use. Apply to affected area 2-3 times a day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin as its active constituents. These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and pain associated with frozen shoulder. Curcumin also has analgesic and anti-exudative properties, which can help reduce swelling and stiffness in the affected joint. The antioxidants in Curcuma longa creams can also help reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to the development of frozen shoulder. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Curcuma longa creams may provide relief from the pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for frozen shoulder
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder, 2 tablespoons of ginger powder, and 2 tablespoons of lavender oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
In a separate bowl, mix the turmeric powder, ginger powder, and lavender oil until well combined.
Slowly add the dry ingredients to the melted oil mixture, stirring constantly to avoid lumps.
Pour the mixture into a container, let it cool and solidify, and then use as a topical cream for frozen shoulder relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams contains bioactive constituents like glycoproteins, polysaccharides, and vitamin E that help with frozen shoulder. The glycoproteins in aloe vera have anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and pain in the affected area. Polysaccharides, such as acemannan, stimulate the immune system and promote healing. Vitamin E, an antioxidant, protects the skin and underlying tissues from damage caused by free radicals. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, aloe barbadensis creams may help alleviate symptoms of frozen shoulder.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for frozen shoulder
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 1/4 cup of Aloe barbadensis gel, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 teaspoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt beeswax in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval until melted.
Combine melted beeswax, coconut oil, and vitamin E oil in a bowl. Stir well to mix.
Add Aloe barbadensis gel to the bowl and stir well to mix. If too thick, add a little distilled water.
Pour mixture into a container, let it cool and solidify. Your Aloe barbadensis cream for frozen shoulder is now ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum creams contains capsaicin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling associated with frozen shoulder. Capsaicin blocks the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain. The creams also contain glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation and promote healing. The capsaicin in Capsicum annuum creams increases blood flow to the affected area, which helps to reduce stiffness and promote mobility. Regular application of Capsicum annuum creams may help reduce pain and inflammation, making it easier to move the shoulder and regain mobility.
remedies.image_alt: Capsicum annuum herbal creams for frozen shoulder
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Capsicum annuum (sweet peppers), 2 cups of coconut oil, 1 cup of shea butter, 1/2 cup of beeswax, and 10 drops of peppermint essential oil.
Peel and chop the Capsicum annuum into small pieces, then blend them in a blender until smooth.
In a double boiler, melt the coconut oil and shea butter over low heat.
Add the chopped Capsicum annuum, beeswax, and peppermint essential oil to the melted coconut oil mixture. Stir well.
Remove the double boiler from heat and let the mixture cool. Once cooled, whip the mixture with a hand mixer until it thickens into a creamy texture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams contains rosmarinic acid, borneol, and camphor as its main bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help reduce pain and swelling associated with frozen shoulder. Rosmarinic acid, in particular, has potent antioxidant properties that may help reduce oxidative stress and promote healing in the affected tissues. The camphor content in Rosmarinus officinalis creams can also help relax muscles and improve blood flow to the area, which can aid in the recovery process. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Rosmarinus officinalis creams may help alleviate symptoms of frozen shoulder, such as stiffness and limited mobility.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for frozen shoulder
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 10 drops of rosemary essential oil, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler. Stir until smooth.
Add the melted beeswax to the coconut oil mixture and stir until combined.
Add the rosemary essential oil and vitamin E oil to the mixture. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify. Use as a cream for frozen shoulder relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitis vinifera creams contains a mix of bioactive constituents, including flavonoids and phenolic acids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the affected area of the shoulder. The antioxidants in Vitis vinifera creams can also help repair damaged tissues and promote collagen production, contributing to the healing process. Additionally, Vitis vinifera creams contain saponins, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve joint mobility. By reducing inflammation and promoting tissue repair, Vitis vinifera creams may help alleviate symptoms of frozen shoulder.
remedies.image_alt: Vitis vinifera herbal creams for frozen shoulder
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Vitis vinifera oil and 1/2 cup of coconut oil in a small saucepan.
Heat the oils over low heat (around 100°F to 110°F) for 10 to 15 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to the saucepan. Stir until melted.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool for 5 minutes. Then, add 2 teaspoons of vitamin E oil and 2 teaspoons of lavender oil.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool completely before applying it to the affected area of your frozen shoulder.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent frozen shoulder. Turmeric and ginger teas are known for their anti-inflammatory properties, which could reduce pain and swelling. Some people also find relief from arnica and willow bark teas. While these teas may help, it's essential to maintain good posture and take regular breaks to avoid straining your shoulder muscles.
faq_safe: Consuming herbal teas for frozen shoulder every day can be safe, but it's essential to choose teas that are gentle on your body. Some herbal teas, like ginger and turmeric, have anti-inflammatory properties that may help relieve pain and stiffness. However, be cautious with teas that can interact with medications or have side effects, such as increased bleeding risk from certain herbs.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas may help with frozen shoulder symptoms, but results vary. Some people notice relief after a few weeks of drinking them regularly, while others may take a couple of months. It's also possible that symptoms may not improve at all. The key is consistency and patience with the herbal teas.
faq_time: For frozen shoulder, it's best to drink herbal tea in the morning or early afternoon, when your body is most active. Drinking it during these times may help your blood flow and reduce stiffness. You can also try drinking it before a gentle exercise routine, like stretching, to see if it helps with your movement.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications, including those used to treat frozen shoulder. St. John's Wort, for example, can speed up the breakdown of certain medications, making them less effective. Certain teas, like peppermint, may also interact with painkillers, reducing their effectiveness. Be cautious when combining herbal teas with prescription medications.
faq_pregnant: When you're pregnant and have frozen shoulder, some herbal teas might be helpful in easing the pain and stiffness. However, it's best to avoid teas that can cause contractions or affect your baby's development. Ginger and peppermint teas are often considered safe, but it's essential to check the ingredients and brewing instructions carefully to ensure they're prepared correctly.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a soothing option for frozen shoulder, but it's a good idea to check the ingredients if you're breastfeeding. Some teas, like those containing sage or peppermint, might affect milk supply. Stick to teas like chamomile, ginger, or fennel that are generally considered safe for breastfeeding mothers and frozen shoulder relief.
title_2: 10 herbal creams for frozen shoulder relief in seniors
